mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer demand momentum; higher sales directly translate into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month changes remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rising-sales months.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates above 8% signal slack that implies future hiring and earnings recovery as the economy reverts toward full employment; this mean-reversion expectation produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 8%, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) coincident with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) compresses real rates and raises input cost pressures before monetary policy can respond, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 60% of joint-signal months, or the joint z-score Rank IC is above zero over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) predicts positive 5-day S&P 500 returns specifically during post-recession recovery phases when manufacturing capacity is rebuilding and operating leverage is high; outside these regimes the relationship breaks down.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth fails to predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 during post-recession recovery periods, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which lifts expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 600 thousand units (HOUST) indicate robust residential investment that multiplies through construction employment and durable goods demand, lifting cyclical earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 600, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or negative returns occur in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in unemployment (UNRATE) and decreases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) confirm labor market contraction that reduces household income and triggers rapid risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of coincident-signal instances, or the mean return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Effective federal funds rates below 0.25% reflect accommodative monetary policy that suppresses risk-free discount rates and incentivizes equity risk-taking, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of such months.
